reasoning: John Fetterman has explicitly stated he will not resign, and his personal brand is built on defying his political critics and being stubborn in the face of left-wing or intra-party backlash. While the background articles from mid-May 2025 suggest he is isolated and angry amidst "article blowback," making a drastic move like formally leaving the Democratic caucus (and thereby risking committee assignments, which requires planning and negotiation with Republicans) or resigning within the roughly 30 days before the July 1 deadline is unlikely. Defections and resignations typically happen over a longer timeline even in the face of pressure. Therefore, despite the apparent rift, the extremely short time horizon and his refusal to resign heavily weigh against him ceasing to caucus with Democrats before July 1.
